title: allow Write(*) + deny Write(*) -> deny wins wherever it matches; Edit is covered by neither (stays ask)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/b-deny-beats-allow/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The file write was blocked. The system has restricted write permissions for this directory as part of the permission precedence test case.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p2b-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: File creation blocked by permission system. The target path is outside the allowed working directory.

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/b-deny-beats-allow/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The system requires approval for the mkdir and file creation operations. Please approve to continue.

probes.prompt: First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/b-deny-beats-allow/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/01-permission/P2-allow-deny-precedence/b-deny-beats-allow/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.
probes.result_text: File read, edit triggered permission request.

hypothesis: deny is evaluated before allow; deny wins wherever it matches, and the match scope of Write(*) is measured across cwd / $HOME / subdir. Rules are tool-scoped: Edit is covered by neither allow nor deny, so it falls back to default ask. Uses the WORKING Write(*) form (Write(**) and path-specific forms silently fail to match -- see P3).
